summary: A BBC Radio 4 podcast description outlining Hannah Arendt's political philosophy, her examination of totalitarianism, and her concept of 'the banality of evil' developed during the Eichmann trial.

mental_models.name: The banality of evil
mental_models.description: A lens used to examine the nature of evil and the actions of individuals who organize and execute atrocities within totalitarian regimes.
mental_models.example: Applied when covering the trial of Adolf Eichmann, one of the organizers of the Holocaust.

signature_quotes.context: Written when covering the trial of Eichmann, one of the organisers of the Holocaust.
